title: Transcribe.cpp Family Intake
description: Machine-readable research packet produced before converter or C++ work begins. See docs/porting/1a-intake.md for field semantics.

family.description: Stable family key used across docs, envs, manifests, tolerances. Examples: parakeet, cohere, qwen3_asr.

hf_revision.description: Commit SHA at the moment of intake. Null only if the source is not on HF; otherwise pin to an exact SHA, not a branch name.

sources.description: Where the draft intake's mechanical facts came from. Manual intakes should still name the files, APIs, or reference code used.

kind.description: Source type, for example hf_file, hf_api, reference_code, manual.

path.description: File path, API name, or source label.

architecture_candidates.description: Heuristic matches against known patterns. Human selects one in architecture_pattern.

key_fields.description: Selected config.json values driving sizing and shape. Free-form key/value map.

varying_across_variants.description: Config keys whose values differ across variants in the same family.

expected.description: Reviewed expected compute/storage dtype for the first accuracy GGUF, e.g. bfloat16, float16, or float32. Null when unresolved.

source.description: Where dtype.expected came from. 'weights_header' means the script inferred it from safetensors header metadata. 'manual' is normal for NeMo/author-repo models. 'unresolved' requires intake signoff to fill or explicitly accept the gap.

evidence.description: Human-readable basis for dtype.expected, for example config.torch_dtype=bfloat16, safetensors header counts, or a reference-code note.

details.description: Optional machine-readable evidence used by the script. Preflight consumes dtype.expected, not these details.

expected_f32_tensors.description: Tensor names or glob patterns expected to remain F32 in a non-F32 model (e.g. norms, biases). Filled by human.

window.description: Window type. 'hann_periodic' and 'hann_symmetric' are a classic mismatch source.

dither.description: Epsilon at inference; typically 0.0 or null.

center.description: STFT center=True pads signal, center=False does not. Affects frame count.

special_tokens.description: Mapping of token role to ID. Omit roles the model doesn't use.

vocab_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the canonical token list. Detects silent vocab drift.

capabilities.description: What the publisher says the model can do. Mirrored into the golden manifest; cross-checked by preflight against GGUF stt.capability.* KVs.

languages.description: BCP-47 language codes the model transcribes. Single element for monolingual. Order should match the upstream model card.

language_detection.description: Model auto-detects input language without a hint.

translation.description: Model produces output in a different language than the input audio.

translation_target_languages.description: Output language codes accepted for translation. Omit or leave empty when translation is false or unknown.

translation_pairs.description: Allowed translation directions as src>target. Only needed when support is not a simple source-language x target-language cross product.

timestamps.description: Granularity of timestamps the model can emit. Empty or ["none"] means transcript-only.

streaming.description: Model supports streaming / chunked real-time inference.

upstream_benchmarks.description: Publisher-claimed benchmarks from the model card or upstream paper. Informational; not a port contract. Our own measured numbers live in docs/models/<family>.md. Empty array is fine when the publisher doesn't report scores.

dataset.description: Dataset name as the publisher reports it. E.g. 'LibriSpeech test-clean', 'Common Voice 17 en', 'FLEURS fr'.

language.description: BCP-47 code of the evaluation language. Null when multilingual aggregate or language doesn't apply.

score.description: Publisher-reported value. Null means 'not reported / not applicable' (e.g. monolingual model has no entry for a foreign-language dataset). Record the number as reported; don't normalize units.

score_unit.description: Unit as reported (e.g. 'ratio' for 0.0168, 'percent' for 1.68). Omit or set null if the metric's natural unit is unambiguous.

source.description: Where the number came from — 'model card', paper URL, blog post URL, etc.

reference_framework.description: One of: nemo, transformers, author_repo_<name>. See 1-reference-research.md decision table.

reference_rationale.description: Why this framework over the alternatives. Human-written.

known_risks.description: Free-form list of risks surfaced by reading the reference code: novel ops, custom attention, multimodal fusion, streaming, long-sequence degradation.

intake_gaps.description: Fields that could not be mechanically determined, with an explanation.
